$comment: Phase 2 plan JSON. Read docs/progress/webapp-rewrite.md §6 + §10 before editing. Generators MAY ONLY flip `status` on existing features. Adding/removing features or changing acceptance criteria is the planner's job (the human or a Planner agent under explicit instruction). The contract reference at the top is the immutable anchor — if you find yourself wanting to change it, you're probably solving the wrong problem.
name: webapp-rewrite

backend.description: FastAPI endpoints in apps/web/. Most logic already exists in dirt_shared.services.{plants, humidifier_state, system_status, plant_detail, wiki, mock_sensors, grow_state, readings, snapshots} — endpoints are thin wrappers that call get_*_payload() composites and shape the response per the contract.

frontend.description: Vite + React + TanStack Router v1 + Tailwind v4 in web-ui/. Use the typed client from web-ui/src/api-client/index.ts (NEVER call fetch directly — TS-05 enforces). Reference packs are mandatory reading: docs/references/{tanstack-router-v1,modern-idiomatic-typescript,tailwind-v4}/INDEX.md.

acceptance_kinds.unit: uv run pytest <path> -q passes (per-app suite or specific test).
acceptance_kinds.invariant: uv run pytest apps/tests/invariants/ -q passes — including test_api_contract.py once EXPECTED_MISSING entry is removed.
acceptance_kinds.lint: cd web-ui && pnpm lint passes (Biome + ESLint with all TS-XX rules).
acceptance_kinds.knip: cd web-ui && pnpm knip passes (no unused files / exports / deps).
acceptance_kinds.build: cd web-ui && pnpm build produces a dist/ tree without errors.
acceptance_kinds.vitest: cd web-ui && pnpm test passes (unit tests for components / lib).
acceptance_kinds.agent-browser: DEPRECATED after frontend.e2e.setup — use `e2e` instead. Legacy entries already pointing at docs/plans/evaluator-checks/*.sh remain valid for done features (frontend.app.shell, frontend.login); pending features should migrate to `e2e` as they come up. Semantics: driving the live :8001 stack with the script at acceptance.script must satisfy every assertion the script encodes; no console errors; expected network requests observed.
acceptance_kinds.e2e: Implementer writes a Playwright spec at acceptance.test_file (typed TypeScript in web-ui/tests/e2e/). `pnpm test:e2e -- <file>` must pass against a live `pnpm dev` on :5173. Evaluator runs it AND audits coverage: every assertion named in acceptance.description must have a corresponding test case in the spec. Missing coverage → fail with escalation='generator' naming the uncovered assertion. See docs/plans/generator-prompts.md and orchestrator.md for the implementer-writes-test convention + critic's coverage-audit step.
acceptance_kinds.visual: Rendered output matches the reference screenshot under acceptance.reference_screenshot to within the pixel-diff threshold. Default: ≤1% pass / 1-3% inspect / >3% fail. Per-feature override: set acceptance[].threshold_pct to a number (e.g. 8) to widen the pass band to ≤N%; inspect runs N–2N; fail is >2N. Use for data-driven visuals where the reference and live render will diverge in pixel-level details (gauge needle positions, sparkline shapes) but the component-level layout must still match.

feature_status_legend.in_progress: Generator worktree exists; status flipped on first commit in the worktree.
feature_status_legend.done: Merged into main; invariants + evaluator agree.
feature_status_legend.blocked: Evaluator failed; see evaluator_notes on the feature for the failure mode and proposed next step.

$features_authoring_note: Each backend feature lists its contract endpoints and the service composite it should thread through. Each frontend feature points at its route + components and lists its API client calls. depends_on captures: (a) BE features that block the FE feature consuming them, (b) intra-lane sequencing where one feature's components are reused by another. Acceptance always includes the contract-invariant unblock (remove the matching EXPECTED_MISSING entry) for backend features.

features.user_story: Operator lands on /, sees the dirt. brand + tab strip (Dashboard / Live / Wiki) + theme toggle + log-out. Tabs route via TanStack Router; theme persists via shared/storage.ts.

acceptance.description: open /, snapshot top bar, assert brand + 3 tab buttons + theme toggle render; click each tab and assert URL + active-tab class change.

features.user_story: Wire MSW v2 into the dev + test pipelines so FE features can build in parallel with their BE counterparts. Each future FE feature registers an endpoint handler against a typed fixture; dev-mode Vite (:5173) serves the mock via a browser Service Worker; prod build (served by FastAPI on :8001) tree-shakes MSW entirely so production hits real APIs. No per-feature wire-up step at BE merge time — the same component code talks to the mock in dev and the real endpoint in prod.
features.implementation_notes: Read docs/references/msw-v2/INDEX.md in full before writing any code. (a) Install msw as a devDependency. (b) Run `pnpm exec msw init web-ui/public --save` ONCE; commit the generated web-ui/public/mockServiceWorker.js (required for browser-mode; not gitignored; regenerated only on MSW major upgrades). (c) handlers.ts exports a `handlers: RequestHandler[]` array that starts empty — future FE features append their handlers here. (d) browser.ts: `setupWorker(...handlers)` from msw/browser. server.ts: `setupServer(...handlers)` from msw/node. (e) main.tsx: wrap the existing ReactDOM.createRoot render in an `enableMocking()` call that, gated by `if (import.meta.env.DEV)`, dynamically imports `./mocks/browser` and calls `worker.start({ onUnhandledRequest: 'bypass' })`. The DEV gate + dynamic import are what let the prod bundle tree-shake MSW out — don't statically import the mocks module from main.tsx. (f) Introduce Vitest (the project didn't have it yet): vitest.config.ts extends vite.config + sets up jsdom environment, test.setupFiles pointing at src/test-setup.ts. test-setup.ts does the node-mode MSW lifecycle (listen / resetHandlers / close) with `onUnhandledRequest: 'error'`. (g) Add a new element-type `mocks` to the web-ui/invariants/eslint.config.ts boundaries rule, pinned to `src/mocks/**`. The rule allows ONLY src/main.tsx and test files (src/**/*.test.{ts,tsx} + src/test-setup.ts) to import from mocks; ui/routes/api-client/shared/features MUST NOT. Because web-ui/invariants is human-owned, describe the eslint.config.ts change precisely in NOTES.md — do NOT edit the invariants file directly; surface this as a planner-escalation item if the invariant needs to change.

acceptance.description: Prod bundle MUST tree-shake MSW out. Verify: `grep -rli 'mock-service-worker\|msw' web-ui/dist/` returns no matches (or only the mockServiceWorker.js copy in dist/, which is a static asset not referenced by the bundle).

Smoke test at web-ui/src/mocks/__tests__/handlers.test.ts: register an http.get handler for /api/__smoke, fetch it, assert the mocked body. Confirms handlers.ts + server.ts wiring are correct and Vitest + MSW node-mode are live.

Demonstrate the boundaries rule rejects a forbidden import: add a throwaway file under web-ui/src/ui/ that imports from src/mocks/ and confirm `pnpm lint` fails with the expected boundaries error; then remove the throwaway file. Record the lint error message in NOTES.md as evidence. (Alternatively, if the planner has landed the invariant edit out-of-band, just confirm the forbidden-import pattern fails lint.)

features.user_story: Wire Playwright into the FE acceptance pipeline so every future FE feature's agent-browser acceptance is a typed .spec.ts test file owned by the implementer and verified by the evaluator — both 'is green' AND 'covers every assertion from the plan's description'. Replaces the shell-script pattern at docs/plans/evaluator-checks/*.sh.
features.implementation_notes: Install @playwright/test as a devDependency; run `pnpm exec playwright install --with-deps chromium` in the setup or document that operators run it once per clone. playwright.config.ts: testDir: './tests/e2e'; use: { baseURL: 'http://localhost:5173' }; webServer starts `pnpm dev` on :5173 on CI (locally, assume the dev server is running). Add `test:e2e` + `test:e2e:ui` scripts to package.json. .gitignore: `test-results/`, `playwright-report/`, `.playwright/`. tests/e2e/_helpers.ts: small shared utilities (goto helpers, form-fill, common selectors). tests/e2e/app-shell.spec.ts: reproduce the assertions in docs/plans/evaluator-checks/app-shell.sh (brand renders, exactly 3 tab buttons, theme toggle, per-tab URL + aria-current=page) as a typed Playwright spec — proves the infrastructure works end-to-end. tests/e2e/README.md: authoring convention for implementer agents — one .spec.ts per feature, one `test(...)` block per plan-JSON assertion from the feature's description, file-scoped fixtures and MSW bridge if needed. The existing .sh files stay as reference; mark them deprecated.

acceptance.description: Run `pnpm test:e2e -- app-shell.spec.ts` against a live `pnpm dev` on :5173. The spec must assert: dirt. brand heading renders; exactly 3 tab buttons with visible text Dashboard/Live/Wiki; a theme-toggle button (aria-label or text matches /theme|light|dark/i); clicking each tab changes location.pathname to /, /live, /wiki respectively; clicked tab carries aria-current='page' on the visible button; page console shows no error-level entries. Green exit.

An existing docs/plans/evaluator-checks/app-shell.sh continues to pass (it's still the evaluator's target for the already-landed frontend.app.shell feature until retirement); this acceptance criterion just confirms the new Playwright spec coexists without breaking the legacy path.

features.user_story: Two coupled concerns that MUST land in one feature because they share the delete-Jinja-root commit: (1) POST /api/auth/login + POST /api/auth/logout + GET /api/auth/me — login sets the dirt_session cookie, logout clears it, me returns the current user or 401. (2) Serve the SPA bundle from apps/web: mount web-ui/dist/assets as StaticFiles, catch-all → web-ui/dist/index.html so TanStack Router can handle any client-side route (including /, /live, /wiki, /login) on refresh and deeplink.
features.implementation_notes: (a) Auth API: reuse SessionManager + AuthMiddleware in apps/web/src/dirt_web/auth.py. Replace the existing Jinja /login GET + form-POST /login + GET /logout routes with the three JSON endpoints. AuthMiddleware changes: gate only /api/* on auth (401 JSON on fail, no more 302 redirect). /api/auth/login, /api/auth/logout, /api/auth/me are carved out as public via security: [] (already in YAML). Every non-/api/* path falls through to SPA serving — auth status is checked client-side via GET /api/auth/me and the SPA routes the user to its own /login screen when 401. (b) SPA serving: delete the @app.get('/') Jinja handler at apps/web/src/dirt_web/app.py. Replace with (i) app.mount('/assets', StaticFiles(directory=<web-ui-dist>/assets), name='spa-assets') and (ii) a catch-all @app.get('/{full_path:path}') → FileResponse(<web-ui-dist>/index.html, media_type='text/html') registered AFTER all /api/* routers so FastAPI's route resolution hits the API first. If web-ui/dist/ is missing at startup (fresh clone without pnpm build), log a WARN and return a 503 placeholder from the catch-all — don't crash. Drop TEMPLATES_DIR, templates/, and the Jinja dependency wiring. The dist directory path comes from a Settings field (default: <repo>/web-ui/dist); tests can point it at a tmp_path fixture that contains a minimal index.html + assets/ layout.

acceptance.description: EXPECTED_MISSING entries for the three /api/auth/* endpoints removed; LEGACY_ROUTES entries for /login GET+POST, /logout GET, / GET removed (deletion of the Jinja routes is part of this feature).

With a tmp_path dist/ containing index.html + assets/main.js: GET / → index.html content; GET /live → same index.html (catch-all for client routes); GET /assets/main.js → the asset bytes; GET /api/unknown → 404 JSON (not the SPA shell — API paths must not fall through); GET /api/auth/me without cookie → 401 JSON (not a 302 redirect).

features.user_story: Pre-auth /login route renders the botanical split-screen form. Submit calls POST /api/auth/login; success routes to /; 401 shows an error. Field-notes block (grow / day / plants / loc / agent) is hardcoded copy — no API call.

acceptance.description: open /login, snapshot, assert hardcoded field-notes block; submit with bad creds → 401 visible; submit with good creds → routed to /; cookie set.

features.user_story: GET /api/grow/current returns germination_date / day_number / grow_week_number / flower_week_number / stage / strain / location / plant_count / lights for the current grow.
features.implementation_notes: Thread GrowStateService.get_grow_current_payload() — already returns the contract shape. Just serialize.

acceptance.description: EXPECTED_MISSING entry for /api/grow/current removed.

features.user_story: GET /api/sensors/current returns the five-metric envelope with target bands, statuses, and a stale flag.
features.implementation_notes: Compose ReadingsService.get_latest_reading + grow_state.STAGE_TARGETS + grow_state.band_status. fan_pct / reservoir_in come from the pure helpers in mock_sensors.py (get_fan_pct / get_reservoir_in) — no provider class, just stateless functions keyed on ts.

acceptance.description: EXPECTED_MISSING entry for /api/sensors/current removed; LEGACY_ROUTES entry for /sensors/current removed.

features.user_story: GET /api/sensors/history?range=&metric= returns bucketed (ts, value) points for one metric over the requested range.
features.implementation_notes: ReadingsService already has _BUCKET_SQL; new endpoint queries one metric at a time per the contract. Old /api/sensors/readings stays until the FE migrates fully, then the legacy entry is removed.

acceptance.description: EXPECTED_MISSING entry for /api/sensors/history removed; LEGACY_ROUTES entry for /api/sensors/readings removed once the FE no longer references it.

features.user_story: Dashboard shows the five gauges (temp / humidity / VPD / fan / reservoir) with arc target bands and ok/warn/crit colour status. Top bar displays Day {day_number} · {strain}.

acceptance.description: Open /, assert the five gauge tiles render with headings matching the metrics from GET /api/sensors/current (temperature, humidity, VPD, fan, reservoir). Assert each tile's displayed value matches the MSW-fixture response value for that metric. Assert target-band arc elements are rendered only on temp/humidity/VPD (not on fan/reservoir). Assert each tile's status indicator color maps to band_status(value, band) → ok|warn|crit. Assert the top bar shows Day {day_number} · {strain} from GET /api/grow/current.

features.user_story: Below the gauges, five sparklines (one per metric) share a crosshair hover and respond to a 1h/24h/7d range switcher.

acceptance.description: Open /. Assert five sparklines render under the gauges (one per metric: temperature, humidity, VPD, fan, reservoir). Click 1h / 24h / 7d in turn in the range switcher and assert that changing the range triggers a fresh GET /api/sensors/history per metric per range change (5 fetches). Hovering one sparkline draws a crosshair shared across all 5 (same x-position marker visible on every sparkline). The value tooltip on hover shows the correct unit suffix per metric (°C / % / kPa / % / in).

features.user_story: GET /api/humidifier/state returns current on/off + duration since last transition + cycles_24h.
features.implementation_notes: HumidifierStateService.get_state already exists; thread it through.

acceptance.description: EXPECTED_MISSING entry for /api/humidifier/state removed.

features.user_story: GET /api/humidifier/history?range= returns the on/off transition list for the requested range.
features.implementation_notes: HumidifierStateService.get_history already exists.

acceptance.description: EXPECTED_MISSING entry for /api/humidifier/history removed.

features.user_story: Humidifier tile shows ON/OFF state + duration since last transition + cycles/24h. Below the sparklines, a duty-cycle strip renders the on/off transitions for the active range.

acceptance.description: Open /. Assert the humidifier tile renders with the ON/OFF state from GET /api/humidifier/state, the duration-since-last-transition text, and the cycles-per-24h count. Below the sparklines, assert the duty-cycle strip renders on/off rectangles sourced from GET /api/humidifier/history — one rectangle per transition. Changing the range via the shared 1h/24h/7d switcher triggers a fresh GET /api/humidifier/history and re-renders the strip.

features.user_story: GET /api/plants returns A–D with current calibrated moisture.
features.implementation_notes: PlantsService.list_plants + per-plant moisture join. Composite already shaped per contract.

acceptance.description: EXPECTED_MISSING entry for /api/plants removed.

features.user_story: Plants strip below the sparklines shows four cards (A/B/C/D) with sticker chip + soil-moisture bar. Click opens the plant-detail drawer.

acceptance.description: Open /. Assert four plant cards render (A, B, C, D) below the sparklines. Each card shows its sticker chip color sourced from GET /api/plants (the response's sticker color per plant). Each card shows a soil-moisture bar whose width matches the plant's moisture.current_pct. Clicking plant A's card opens the plant-detail drawer (the drawer is rendered by frontend.plant_detail, lands in a later feature — for this spec, assert the click triggers a GET /api/plants/a, no need to assert drawer contents).

features.user_story: GET /api/plants/{code} returns the full drawer payload (label / moisture / timeline / note / wiki_path).
features.implementation_notes: PlantDetailService.get(code) parses wiki/plants/plant-{code}.md frontmatter + Timeline + Current State. Result shaped per contract.

acceptance.description: EXPECTED_MISSING entry for /api/plants/{code} removed.

features.user_story: GET /api/plants/{code}/moisture?range= returns bucketed moisture points + irrigation_events_24h count.
features.implementation_notes: PlantsService.get_plant_moisture_history + irrigation event heuristic (count of upward steps ≥ N%).

acceptance.description: EXPECTED_MISSING entry for /api/plants/{code}/moisture removed.

features.user_story: Plant-detail drawer overlays the dashboard with header (sticker + name + primary/secondary tag), moisture hero (current + target band visualization), 24h moisture chart, timeline list, and current-status note. Drop the lint ✓ badge.

acceptance.description: Open /. Click plant A's card in the plants strip. Assert the drawer opens (a dialog/aside element becomes visible). Assert the drawer's frontmatter rows render (sticker, name, primary/secondary tag) matching GET /api/plants/a. Assert the moisture hero displays moisture.current_pct matching the response. Assert the timeline list's row count equals response.timeline.length. Pressing ESC closes the drawer (dialog becomes hidden).

features.user_story: GET /api/system/devices returns the 8-row device status table.
features.implementation_notes: SystemStatusService.get_device_statuses; status taxonomy ok|listening|warn|offline. Jabra status is mocked from systemctl is-active dirt-voice for now.

acceptance.description: EXPECTED_MISSING entry for /api/system/devices removed.

features.user_story: Dashboard system section renders the device table.

acceptance.description: Open /. Assert the system section renders a table with 8 rows, one per device from GET /api/system/devices. Each row's status badge visually reflects the device's status value (ok|listening|warn|offline) — use an accessible indicator (role/aria-label or textContent) rather than color-only.

features.user_story: GET /api/feed/live.jpg returns one live JPEG via the camera daemon.
features.implementation_notes: Reuse capture_frame() from dirt_shared.services.capture; rename current /feed/live route. Set Cache-Control: no-store.

acceptance.description: EXPECTED_MISSING entry for /api/feed/live.jpg removed; LEGACY_ROUTES entries for /feed/live, /feed/image, /feed/status removed.

features.user_story: GET /api/feed/snapshot/latest returns the most recent archived snapshot.
features.implementation_notes: Rename /api/snapshots/latest; reuse SnapshotsService.latest + get_snapshot_path.

acceptance.description: EXPECTED_MISSING entry for /api/feed/snapshot/latest removed; LEGACY_ROUTES entry for /api/snapshots/latest removed.

features.user_story: GET /api/ptz/state returns connected / yaw / pitch / zoom / preset / presets[] with yaw/pitch/zoom per preset.
features.implementation_notes: RPC the dirt-camera daemon socket via dirt_shared.services.capture._daemon_rpc('get_state'). Preset list comes from ~/.config/dirt/camera.json — augment that file with yaw/pitch/zoom per preset if not already present.

acceptance.description: EXPECTED_MISSING entry for /api/ptz/state removed.

features.user_story: POST /api/ptz/preset/{id} moves the camera to a preset and returns the new yaw/pitch/zoom.
features.implementation_notes: Call _daemon_rpc('look_preset', id). 404 on unknown preset (validate against camera.json).

acceptance.description: EXPECTED_MISSING entry for /api/ptz/preset/{id} removed.

features.user_story: POST /api/ptz/look {x, y} re-centers via normalized click. Server applies yaw_delta = x*60°, pitch_delta = y*40°.
features.implementation_notes: Compute new motor-frame yaw/pitch from current state, clamp to daemon limits, call _daemon_rpc('look_at_motor_xy', yaw, pitch).

acceptance.description: EXPECTED_MISSING entry for /api/ptz/look removed.

features.user_story: POST /api/ptz/zoom — exactly one of {zoom, delta}; absolute or relative.
features.implementation_notes: Validate XOR; call _daemon_rpc('zoom_to', value) or 'zoom_by'.

acceptance.description: EXPECTED_MISSING entry for /api/ptz/zoom removed.

features.user_story: Live tab shows the camera feed (auto-refresh 10s), click-to-look on the feed, presets list (overview + plant_a..d), zoom slider 1–4×.

acceptance.description: Navigate to /live. Assert an img element renders whose src targets /api/feed/live.jpg (the MSW handler returns a small test JPEG blob). Assert the img's src is refreshed (e.g. via a cache-busting query param change) within ~10s. Clicking on the feed at a normalized (x, y) fires POST /api/ptz/look with x and y in [-1, 1] — the spec can force-click at known canvas coords and assert the request body. Clicking a preset row in the presets list fires POST /api/ptz/preset/{id} with the matching id. Dragging the zoom slider fires POST /api/ptz/zoom with the new value (or a debounced sequence ending at the drop position).

features.user_story: GET /api/wiki/tree walks wiki/ on disk and returns a folder/file tree with titles + plant sticker colors.
features.implementation_notes: Use WikiService.get_tree. ETag from rollup mtime hash.

acceptance.description: EXPECTED_MISSING entry for /api/wiki/tree removed.

features.user_story: GET /api/wiki/file?path= returns frontmatter + raw markdown body + backlinks for a single wiki file.
features.implementation_notes: WikiService.get_file. Path normalization accepts foo/bar.md or wiki/foo/bar.md; reject any path that escapes wiki/.

acceptance.description: EXPECTED_MISSING entry for /api/wiki/file removed.

features.user_story: GET /api/wiki/search?q= returns ranked results with match_type and optional snippet. 400 on empty q.
features.implementation_notes: WikiService.search; substring scan over filenames + bodies for V1.

acceptance.description: EXPECTED_MISSING entry for /api/wiki/search removed.

features.user_story: Wiki tab: sidebar tree + main pane renders raw markdown with frontmatter block + breadcrumb. Cmd+K opens search palette; empty state shows recent files (client-side via shared/storage.ts). Drop the lint ✓ footer badge.

acceptance.description: Navigate to /wiki. Assert the sidebar tree renders folders + files from GET /api/wiki/tree. Click plant-a.md in the sidebar: the main pane renders a frontmatter block (matter rows) and the markdown body from GET /api/wiki/file?path=plants/plant-a.md. Press Cmd+K: a search palette becomes visible. Type 'topping' into the palette input: GET /api/wiki/search?q=topping fires and match results render. Press Enter on a match: URL navigates (e.g. to /wiki?path=…). Open palette with an empty query: recent files render from shared/storage.ts AND no GET /api/wiki/search with empty q is fired (assert via network tracking).

runbook_for_evaluator: Verify the harness smoke test passes (see webapp-rewrite.md §0).
uv run pytest -q must be green.
cd web-ui && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm knip && pnpm build must all pass.

For each `done`-pending feature: run its acceptance.script under agent-browser against the live :8001 stack; flip status to done if every assertion passes and there are no console errors; flip to blocked + write evaluator_notes if any assertion fails.
After each merge into main, re-verify all four systemd services (dirt-hwd, dirt-web, dirt-camera, dirt-voice) are still active.
runbook_for_planner: Adding a new endpoint: edit contracts/webapp-v1.yaml first; run scripts/gen-contract; add (path, method) to EXPECTED_MISSING in apps/tests/invariants/test_api_contract.py with a feature_id; add the matching feature to this plan; run pytest invariants to confirm everything is wired.
Adding a new feature: append to features[] with at minimum {id, lane, depends_on, user_story, acceptance[], status:'pending'}.
Removing a feature: never. Mark it done if shipped, blocked if abandoned, with evaluator_notes explaining why.
